{{Quote|I hate working with amateurs.|Jade, a Mikoan mercenary|<ref name="Poly" />}}
The '''Mikoans''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] native to [[Miko]]. They were [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]], and appeared [[Human/Legends|Human]], bar their [[Green (disambiguation)|green]] skin pigmentation. The Mikoans resisted the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] until their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was [[First Battle of Miko|bombarded]] by [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Delurin]] from aboard the prototype [[Dragon-class Heavy Cruiser|''Dragon''-class heavy cruiser]]. That overwhelming show of force compelled them to surrender. However, they were saved when Delurin abandoned the [[Star system/Legends|star system]] to establish his own splinter faction of the Empire, as the "[[Warlord of Wild Space]]." The Mikoans were again [[Second Battle of Miko|attacked]] by Delurin just as they were about to join the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].

A notable Mikoan was [[Jade (mercenary)|Jade]], a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] who did some work for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. She played a role in ensuring that the Alliance received intelligence that alerted them to the presence of the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] aboard the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] at the time of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Mikoans were a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that could easily pass for [[Human/Legends|Human]] but for their [[Green (disambiguation)|green]] skin. The Mikoan [[Jade (mercenary)|Jade]] had green [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and [[Color|black]] hair. Members of the species could grow to 1.8 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height.<ref name="Poly" />

==History==
[[File:Delurin.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Delurin twice attacked the Mikoan homeworld.]]
The Mikoans were native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Miko]] of the [[Sorella system/Legends|Sorella system]].<ref name="Dragon">{{DragonCite|200|The Galaxy Dragon and crew|The ''Galaxy Dragon'' and crew}}</ref> It was located within the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]]' [[Tapani sector/Legends|Tapani sector]], part of the [[Three Ellas Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] between the [[Dorella system/Legends|Dorella]] and [[Tavya system/Legends|Tavya systems]]. While the Tapani sector was largely dominated by the powerful [[Tapani Noble Houses]], the Mikoan [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was part of the [[Freeworlds Territory]], which had a degree of independence from the Noble Houses.<ref name="Lords of the Expanse">''[[Lords of the Expanse]]''</ref>

In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Mikoans refused to submit to the authority of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. In retaliation for their insubordination, [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Delurin]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] was dispatched to subdue the recalcitrant humanoids. Delurin, who was overseeing the final test run of a prototype [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] known as the [[Dragon-class Heavy Cruiser|''Dragon''-class heavy cruiser]], took the ship directly to the Sorella system and ordered the crew to open fire on [[City/Legends|cities]] on Miko's surface. The Mikoans initially resisted as [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] fire and [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]] rained on their planet. However, they eventually sued for peace, begging Delurin to cease his [[First Battle of Miko|assault]]. Just as he was concluding the bombardment, Delurin received word of the [[Death/Legends|death]] of the Emperor at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Ignoring orders to regroup in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], Delurin left Miko to form his own splinter territory from the Empire, declaring himself the "[[Warlord of Wild Space]]."<ref name="Dragon" />

Following the departure of Delurin, the Mikoans considered joining the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. As diplomatic negotiations were about to successfully conclude, Delurin returned to the [[Star system/Legends|star system]] with his [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]], christened the ''[[Galaxy Dragon]]''. Psychologically scarred by the previous bombardment, the Mikoans were frozen with indecision at his arrival, leaving the New Republic diplomatic corps and a small group of special-operatives to rally [[Second Battle of Miko|resistance]] to the [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]].<ref name="Dragon" />

==Mikoans in the galaxy==
A Mikoan named Jade ventured off her homeworld as a young woman, finding work as a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]]. She considered herself unaffiliated during the Galactic Civil War, but took paying jobs from the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. For one such task, Jade was stationed on [[Gellefon]] in the [[Korness system]], where she regularly received information from a faction of [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Spy/Legends|spies]] known as "[[Deep Ear]]." In this capacity, Jade received a high-security transmission with intelligence that the Galactic Emperor, Palpatine, would be aboard the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] over [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] at the time of the pending Alliance assault on the battle station.<ref name="Poly" />

Jade immediately contacted [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] and requested immediate evacuation from Gellefon. However, her message was garbled and the Alliance sent only a small team of operatives, believing the mission to be a routine pick-up of intelligence. Just as Jade was making contact with the Rebels, she was met by [[Borsk Fey'lya]], bearing news of the capture and [[Prison/Legends|imprisonment]] of the Deep [[Ear/Legends|Ear]] team on Gellefon. Jade, Fey'lya and the small group of Rebel operatives linked up with a local resistance movement, the [[Gellefon Freedom Fighters]], and succeeded in breaking out the Bothans from their imprisonment. They then fled the planet and delivered their intelligence to the Alliance commanders.<ref name="Poly" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Mikoans were created by [[Bill Slavicsek]] for the [[roleplaying game]] article "[[The New Republic Campaign: Preparing to Move Beyond the Movies]]," published in ''[[Polyhedron 86]]'' in [[1993]]. At this time, and up until [[2003]], the ''[[Polyhedron]]'' magazine<ref name="Poly" /> was not a [[canon]]ical source.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> In 2003, ''Polyhedron'' became officially licensed, and all issues published since this time are canonical. This is not retrospective, however, so the Mikoans remain outside canon.<ref name="SWBoards">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=222689&start=1575#14229569|thread=Welcome some BCaT VIPs|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2006-02-28|time=6:28 PM}}</ref> In addition to the ''Polyhedron'' article, Slavicsek also included Mikoans in his article "[[The Galaxy Dragon and crew|The ''Galaxy Dragon'' and crew]]," published in ''[[Dragon Magazine 200]]'' later in 1993. [[Dragon (magazine)|''Dragon Magazine'']] is also an unlicensed source. While Slavicsek placed the Mikoan homeworld in a later canonical star-system from ''[[Lords of the Expanse]]'', however, this has no impact on the canonicity of the Mikoans themselves.
